items.title: G.C.P.S.S.M.
items.description: Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music—audio from the live gesture-controlled performance system.
items.fullDescription: G.C.P.S.S.M. (Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music) is part of the Gesture-Controlled Performance System: gesture and speed control rework older songs as samples, extending the live A/V platform into released audio. Released May 2026 on asymmetrica.
items.medium: Digital audio, Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music
items.technology: TouchDesigner, MediaPipe, gesture control, sampling, speed mapping
items.themes: gesture, performance systems, slow music, G.C.P.S.S.M., sample rework

items.title: recording tests at the akerk, groningen
items.description: Field recording tests in the Akerk, Groningen—paired takes with professional and DIY electret microphones.
items.fullDescription: Four long-form recording tests captured in the Akerk, Groningen, comparing AudioTechnica and DIY electret microphone setups in the center hall and left benches. Released March 2026 on asymmetrica.
items.medium: Digital audio, field recording, acoustic space documentation
items.technology: AudioTechnica microphone, DIY electret microphone, church acoustics
items.themes: field recording, acoustic architecture, microphone comparison, Groningen

items.title: -12° C
items.description: Cold, restrained electronic composition shaped by sparse motion.
items.fullDescription: -12° C maintains a reduced palette, favouring brittle textures, low-energy movement, and extended silences between events. The track is structured around restraint, with sounds appearing isolated and exposed rather than layered. Its pacing and tonal temperature suggest an environment where energy is conserved and every action carries weight.
items.technology: Ableton Live, restrained synthesis, minimal sequencing, spatial reverb
items.themes: coldness, restraint, isolation, minimal motion, sonic economy

items.title: Impulse
items.description: Short-form rhythmic study centred on attack and immediacy.
items.fullDescription: Impulse is built from sharp transient events and tightly gated signals, foregrounding attack as the primary compositional material. The piece avoids development in favour of insistence, pushing a small set of gestures through rapid repetition and abrupt interruption. The result is a compact, physical track that reads more as signal behaviour than song structure.
items.technology: Ableton Live, envelope shaping, transient control, rhythmic gating
items.themes: attack, immediacy, signal behaviour, repetition, interruption

items.title: Sublimatic
items.description: Minimalist electronic structure driven by gradual transformation.
items.fullDescription: Sublimatic unfolds slowly, prioritising temporal stretch over event density. Small shifts in timbre, modulation depth, and spatial placement accumulate across the duration, producing a perceptual drift rather than narrative progression. The track functions as a listening environment, asking attention to recalibrate to subtle changes rather than explicit musical gestures.
items.medium: Digital audio, minimal composition, slow modulation systems
items.technology: Ableton Live, long-form automation, subtractive synthesis, spatial effects
items.themes: gradual change, duration, minimalism, perception, sonic environment

items.title: The Sauce
items.description: Rhythmic distortion study built around pressure, repetition, and release.
items.fullDescription: The Sauce operates as a compressed loop ecology, where distorted percussive gestures fold back onto themselves through tight timing and aggressive filtering. The track leans on repetition as a stress mechanism, gradually thickening its own texture through saturation, micro-variation, and controlled overload. What emerges is a functional groove that feels constantly on the verge of destabilising, yet never fully breaks.
items.medium: Digital audio, loop-based composition, distortion processing
items.technology: Ableton Live, audio effects chains, saturation, filtering, rhythmic automation
items.themes: pressure, repetition, groove collapse, texture, rhythmic insistence

items.title: A Reasonable Crashout
items.description: Frequency modulation study by asymmetrica, balancing chaos and control.
items.fullDescription: A Reasonable Crashout is a deliberately unstable composition—a digital homage to modular synthesis built entirely in Ableton. The track oscillates between glitchy randomness and hypnotic repetition, filled with jittery pattern jumps, unpredictable modulation, and a constantly morphing yet grounded melodic pulse. It's a study in fractured coherence: non-linear, algorithmically warped, but held together by an insistent sense of drive.
items.medium: Digital audio, algorithmic sequencing, Frequency Modulation
items.technology: Ableton Live, randomized MIDI effects, automation envelopes, FM synthesis
items.themes: FM synthesis, intensity, sonic collapse, modularity, chaos, rhythm, generative structure

items.title: Automaton
items.description: An exploration of mechanical repetition and evolving digital patterns.
items.fullDescription: Automaton features repetitive motifs and incremental variations that evoke mechanized processes and evolving synthetic life.

items.title: Biding My Time
items.description: Glitchy percussive experiment by asymmetrica, exploring timing and texture.
items.fullDescription: Biding My Time is a rhythmic study shaped through glitch techniques and Reaktor experiments. It navigates broken beats, digital stutters, and layered percussive fragments, producing an evolving sense of restless suspension.

items.title: Have a Nice Last Few Days of Summer
items.description: Bell-driven experimental textures by asymmetrica, meditative yet fleeting.
items.fullDescription: Have a Nice Last Few Days of Summer centers on bell tones and resonant layers. The piece balances clarity and haze, echoing cycles of memory and transition, like the shimmer of late-season light dissolving into air.

bandcampTracks.title: have a nice last few days of summer

items.title: Not As I Remember It
items.description: A reflective piece examining memory through fragmented sound and abstract textures.
items.fullDescription: Not As I Remember It distorts familiar motifs into abstract forms, questioning the reliability of recollection and the fluidity of sonic memory.

items.title: Stranded Deep Series
items.description: A paired exploration of displacement and rapid flux through digital soundscapes, merging organic resonance and synthetic textures.
items.fullDescription: This pair—'stranded deep' and 'stranded deep but fast'—represents a sonic dive into temporal distortion and emotional flux. These compositions juxtapose slow meditative pulses with accelerated glitch rhythms, reflecting themes of isolation and rapid change.
items.themes: Temporal manipulation, glitch aesthetics, organic-synthetic synthesis

items.title: Stretching
items.description: A sonic exploration of elasticity and temporal extension within digital sound design.
items.fullDescription: Stretching plays with time, slowing and elongating motifs to blur boundaries between moment and duration, creating a meditative auditory space.

items.title: Supersounds
items.description: An experimental dive into layered synthetic timbres and expansive sound fields.
items.fullDescription: Supersounds layers evolving textures and synthetic drones to create a complex soundscape that challenges perception and embraces machine improvisation.
items.themes: Synthetic layering, machine improvisation, drone textures

items.title: Symmetrical Fictions
items.description: A generative composition shaped by real-time weather data, translating environmental conditions into shifting sonic structures.
items.fullDescription: Generative audio from live weather data (temperature, humidity, precipitation), mapped to pitch, modulation, and rhythm—ambient but unstable, synthetic timbres tied to shifting outdoor conditions.
items.themes: Weather, systems, sonic translation, speculative environments

items.title: The Machine
items.description: An exploration of mechanized rhythms and synthetic textures embodying the essence of automated sound production.
items.fullDescription: The Machine evokes industrial soundscapes with tight rhythmic patterns and digital noise, interrogating the relationship between human and machine in sound creation.
items.themes: Industrial aesthetics, mechanized rhythm, synthetic textures

items.title: Tiptoe
items.description: A delicate balance of tension and subtle sonic shifts, tiptoeing between ambient textures and rhythmic pulses.
items.fullDescription: Tiptoe navigates quiet spaces filled with fragile sound detail, exploring the interface between near silence and low-frequency movements. It's an experiment in minimalism and patience.
items.themes: Minimalism, ambient textures, tension and release
